title: hoya australis subsp. melanesica
intro: Hoya australis subsp. melanesica, also known as the Melanesian waxy, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is a climber native to the Pacific Islands, known for its fragrant and waxy flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hoya australis subsp. melanesica include reducing stress, improving sleep, and providing relief from fever and respiratory issues; it can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
